summary: First end-to-end smoke test of Jenish portfolio. Backend (4/4) and frontend (all primary flows) verified against public URL. Contact submission persists to MongoDB; filters, FAQs, nav, hero, mobile nav all functional. One minor deviation: native HTML5 required-validation handles empty contact form instead of a custom Sonner error toast (no POST is fired, so requirement partially satisfied).
passed: POST /api/contact valid payload -> 200 returns saved record with id+ISO timestamp
POST /api/contact invalid email -> 422 with pydantic email validation error
GET /api/contact -> 200, sorted by timestamp desc (latest record on top)
Frontend loads at root, data-testid='portfolio-root' visible, 0 console errors
All 6 nav-link-* testids present and clickable (home/about/skills/projects/faqs/contact)
Hero shows 'Crafting Digital' heading + data-testid='hero-typed-word' animating
Projects: 4 default cards; filter-frontend -> 2; filter-full-stack -> 2; filter-all -> 4
FAQ toggles 0 and 2 clickable without errors
Contact form valid submission: success toast 'Message sent. I will get back within 24 hours.', inputs cleared, record persisted (verified via GET /api/contact)
Mobile nav (390px) opens via nav-menu-toggle and closes via nav-menu-close
Empty contact form does NOT fire POST /api/contact (verified via request listener)

failed.issue: Empty form submission shows the browser's native HTML5 'Please fill out this field' tooltip instead of a Sonner error toast (spec said 'error toast').
failed.evidence: Screenshot shows native validation popover; no [data-sonner-toast] element rendered. POST is correctly suppressed.

action_items: Optional: if a custom Sonner error toast is required for empty fields, remove the 'required' HTML attribute on inputs and rely on JS validation + toast.error(...). Otherwise current behavior is acceptable since no invalid POST is fired.
